exploration_log.aha_moment: The existing Compare(const Cord&) inline fast path calls InlineData::Compare which only accepts InlineData, not string_view. For the string_view case, I can bypass InlineData::Compare and directly use memcmp on the inline chars, replicating the same normalization logic as SharedCompareImpl.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could have added a convenience helper `absl::string_view Cord::InlineRep::as_string_view()` to make the fast path more readable, but that would be a larger change.

alternatives: Expose as_string_view on InlineData and use string_view::compare
key_decisions.decision: Direct memcmp on inline chars with normalized return value
key_decisions.reasoning: InlineData::Compare only accepts InlineData; adding a new overload there would be more invasive. Direct memcmp closely mirrors what string_view::compare would do but normalizes to -1/0/1 to match the Compare contract.

queries.query_text: Add inline non-tree fast path for Cord::Compare(string_view) in cord.h mirroring existing Compare(const Cord&) using CompareImpl slow path

solve_notes: Added an inline fast path for `Cord::Compare(absl::string_view)` in `cord.h` that short-circuits to a direct `memcmp` on the inline chars when `contents_.is_tree()` is false, mirroring the existing `Compare(const Cord&)` optimization. The slow path calls a new `CompareImpl(absl::string_view)` which replaces the old out-of-line `Compare(absl::string_view)` definition in `cord.cc`.
task_text:
The `Cord::Compare(const Cord&)` in `strings/cord.h` has an inline fast
path that directly calls `contents_.data_.Compare()` when both cords are
non-tree (inline) cords. The `Cord::Compare(absl::string_view)` overload
declared in `cord.h` and implemented in `cord.cc` lacks this fast path and
always dispatches through `SharedCompareImpl`, which traverses the full
chunk iterator even for short inline cords. Add an inline non-tree fast
path to `Cord::Compare(absl::string_view)` in `cord.h` mirroring the
existing `Cord::Compare(const Cord&)` optimization.

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers only the edited defs. An AI agent would additionally look at the existing Compare(const Cord&) inline fast path to understand the pattern it must mirror, and at InlineData::as_chars() to know the API for extracting inline bytes. Without seeing these upfront, the agent would search for them during implementation.
